text: INDIAN INSTITUTEOF TECHNOLOGYJAMMU et-*a- ** IITJMU/Acad/2021/232 22 Oct 2021 NOTIFICATION Sub: Guidelines for Utilization of Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Research Grant/Contingency. As per the guidelines of the Ministry of Education for PMRF scholars, a Research grant of Rs.2 lakh per year is provided to a PMRF scholar. Thus a scholar is entitled to get RS.10 lakh during the period of the fellowship, subject to PMRF guidelines. The competent authority has approved the guidelines for utilization of PMRF Grant/Contingency, attached as Annexure-A. Faculty All PIn :+d 9ia 1n - 1in 9s 1t -i 2tu 5t 7e 03o 8f 1T /e 2ch 5n 7e 0l 2o 8g 0y | J Eam : hm elu p, d J ea sg kti @, N iiH tj a4 m4 m, J u.a .atu c.n inu | - w18 :1 w 2 w2 w1, . iJ i& tjK a, m I mun udi .a a. c .in Annexur A tu Notification No. IITIMU/Acad/z021/T% Dated 22 Oct 2021 Guidelines for utilization of PMRF Research Grant/ Contingency 1 As per the guidelines of the Ministry of Education for PMRF scholars, a Research Grant of Rs.2 lakh per year is provided to a PMRF scholar. Thus a scholar is entitled to get upto Rs.10 lakh during the duration of the Fellowship. PMRF scholar can utilize the yearly grant of Rs.2 lakh in an academic year or accurmulats the amount fully or partially over the years and utilize before five years (PhD tenure) or till the date of submission of Ph.D. thesis, whichever is earlier. 2. PMRF scholars can utilize this fund over following expenses in concurrence with the Supervisor and the Head of the Department: a) Support for travel, registration and accommodation to participate in conferences, symposiums, workshops, seminars, training, short-term courses, fieldwork and other programs aiding the research work of the student. b) Support for International Conferences outside India available only after completion of Comprehensive Examination. A PMRF candidate shal not be eligible for Institute support for conferences within India abroad. c)Entitlement oft ravel and accommodation charges would be as per prevailing rules for the PhD students of the IT Jammu. d) Purchase of one PC/ laptop permitted once during the entire fellowship period. e) Purchase

workshops, seminars, training, short-term courses, fieldwork and other programs aiding the research work of the student. b) Support for International Conferences outside India available only after completion of Comprehensive Examination. A PMRF candidate shal not be eligible for Institute support for conferences within India abroad. c)Entitlement oft ravel and accommodation charges would be as per prevailing rules for the PhD students of the IT Jammu. d) Purchase of one PC/ laptop permitted once during the entire fellowship period. e) Purchase of one tablet/e-reader permitted once during the entire fellowship period. f)Purchase of one printer/scanner or all in one, webcam, keyboard and stylus pen permitted once during8 the entire fellowship period. g) Purchase of hard disk, pen drive, CD and mouse as and when required. h) Payment of publication charges in reputed SCI indexed journals including color page charges, extra page charges, payment of PhD thesis submission fee. i) Life membership charges of professional bodies or societies, limited to one membership of national and one international body in an academic year. )Purchaseofb ooks, conference proceedings, e-books, reprints of research articles and journal subscription charges. k) Purchase of non-consumables/ consumables and other items related to their research. Also the followingrules will also be followed: ) Unspent balances in a year shall be carried over to the next academic year. m) All items must be entered in the relevant stock registers of the lab/group/department. n) All bills/ cover notes shall be coutersigned by the PhD supervisor and the Ph.D. student. o) Testing and analysis charges for facilities used within campus will be paid dire ctly by the accounts section to the concerned project in R&D. p) Institute accounting practices/ purchases manual isto be followed for all purchases (Conformity with the GFR- 2017 rules as amended from time to time). g)Bills/Receipts are to be submitted to the accounts section within one month. r)No advance for purchases will be provided. However, travel advance for an international contference outside India may be given subject to a maximum of 80% of the costs involved or the bakanae amount in the grant, whichever is lower. s)Scholars desiring to retain items under (d), (e) and (f) acquired from this grant, may do so at the end or the programme by paying 5% of the acquisition cost to the Institute account (provided it is more than 3 years old). For items less than three years old, the institute depreciation rules

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- UG Programme
- PG Programme
- Ph.D Programme
- PMRF

The Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme has been designed for improving the quality of research in various higher educational institutions in the country. With attractive fellowships, the scheme seeks to attract the best talent into research thereby realizing the vision of development through innovation. The scheme was announced in the Budget 2018-19. The institutes which can offer PMRF include all the IITs, all the IISERs, Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru and some of the top Central Universities/NITs that offer science and/or technology degrees. The candidates will be selected through a rigorous selection process and their performance will be reviewed suitably through a national convention.

**National Coordinating Institute :**IIT Madras

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

### About PMRF

The Prime Minister's Research Fellows (PMRF) Scheme has been designed for improving the quality of research in various higher educational institutions in the country. With attractive fellowships, the scheme seeks to attract the best talent into research thereby realizing the vision of development through innovation. The scheme was announced in the Budget 2018-19. The institutes which can offer PMRF include all the IITs, all the IISERs, Indian Institute of Science, Bengaluru and some of the top Central Universities/NITs that offer science and/or technology degrees. The candidates will be selected through a rigorous selection process and their performance will be reviewed suitably through a national convention.

**National Coordinating Institute :**IIT Madras

### PMRF coordinator

##### Dr. Biswanath Chakraborty
Associate Dean (Research Programs)

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

**SAURABH KUMAR JHA**

E-mail: 2022rmt0023@iitjammu.ac.in

Department: Materials Engineering

Institute: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu

I am a dedicated PhD candidate in Materials Engineering, specializing in pioneering research on polymers materials for extreme cold climates.

With expertise in synthesis and characterization of polymers, I am excited to contribute in the field of Polymer fiber/textiles for strategic application.

**Research description**

My research domain relates to the field of lightweight advanced textiles for extreme cold climate derived from polymer blend which are characterized by distinct properties of both natural and synthetic fibers. The fact that conventional textiles performance deteriorates in subzero cold climate, compelled us to research and develop a novel textile material, which can be comfortably used by Indian armed forces. The proposed textiles are combination of synthetic and natural polymer reinforced with nanoparticles can provide exceptional thermal insulation, durability, comfort and ballistic property. This hybrid fiber can be made using solution blending, which ensures the complete miscibility of PU with silk fiber at molecular level. The hybrid fiber can reduce injuries caused by the cold to armed forces. The crucial factors for fabric design such as moisture management, breathability, warmth, resistance from ice formation, human physiology, are considered under this proposed research.
